statement.en: The DESI Dark Energy Tension as a Boundary Projection Effect
statement.summary: Recent data from the Dark Energy Spectroscopic Instrument sharpen the late-time tension with a strictly constant cosmological term when the expansion history is expressed in standard bulk variables. In the present framework, the quantity governing cosmic acceleration is not an effective bulk dark energy density, but the boundary-normalized horizon relation ΛR_H² = π³/15. When this fixed geometric relation is rewritten in bulk form through R_H = c / H, it gives an effective equation of state, w_eff = −1 − 2Ḣ / (3H²). For the flat background Ω_Λ = π³/45, Ω_m = 1 − π³/45, and H²(a) / H₀² = Ω_m a⁻³ + Ω_Λ, this yields w₀ = −π³/45 ≃ −0.6890 and w_a = 3Ω_ΛΩ_m ≃ 0.6428. These values do not arise from an additional fit, but follow directly from the fixed horizon normalization under spatial flatness

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The proposal introduces a novel geometric boundary condition (ΛR_H² = π³/15) to resolve observational tension in DESI data. This is mathematically self-consistent and generates testable predictions (w₀ ≃ −0.6890, w_a ≃ 0.6428) without free parameters. However, the framework remains provisional: (1) it lacks independent observational validation beyond post-hoc DESI reconciliation, (2) the physical justification for the boundary normalization is geometric rather than derived from fundamental theory, (3) publication date (2026) suggests recent preprint status, (4) no independent replication or cross-validation against other datasets (Planck, SNIa, BAO) is reported in abstract. The work is rigorous within its axiomatic structure but requires longitudinal validation.
